description: Fix shared list store visibility by refactoring store RLS policies to allow read-only access to stores referenced by items in shared lists, and updating both React and Swift clients to fetch and display the list owner's stores.

userStories.title: Refactor Store RLS Policies
userStories.description: Replace the single stores_all_own blanket RLS policy with four granular per-operation policies. The SELECT policy allows reading own stores and stores referenced by items in lists shared with the current user. Add index on items(store_id) for subquery performance.
acceptanceCriteria: The blanket stores_all_own policy is replaced by four granular policies (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
A user can SELECT their own stores (no regression)
A user can SELECT stores belonging to another user when those stores are referenced by items in a list shared with them
A user cannot INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE another user's stores
Index idx_items_store_id exists on items(store_id)
Migration is idempotent (can run on fresh DB or existing)
userStories.testIntensityReason: Security-critical RLS changes — must verify no unauthorized access to other users' stores

userStories.title: React — Fetch Owner's Stores for Shared Lists
userStories.description: Update the store subscription/fetch logic so that when viewing a shared list, the owner's stores are also loaded and available for display. Add fetchStoresByIds() to database.js, merge shared stores in ShoppingListContext, and show owner's stores in the AddItemForm store picker.
acceptanceCriteria: When viewing a shared list, items are grouped under the correct store sections with name, color, and categories
The current user's own stores still load and display correctly (no regression)
Store section headers show the owner's store name and color dot
Store-specific category grouping works (owner's store categories used for item categorization)
Add-item store picker shows both own stores and owner's stores when on a shared list
No duplicate stores appear if both users happen to have stores with the same name
Lint passes
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: React — Realtime Sync for Shared Stores
userStories.description: When the list owner renames, recolors, or modifies categories on a store, those changes should propagate to the shared user's view in realtime. Subscribe to a second realtime channel for the owner's stores when on a shared list.
acceptanceCriteria: When the list owner renames a store, the shared user sees the updated name without refresh
When the list owner changes a store's color, the shared user sees the updated color without refresh
When the list owner modifies store categories, the shared user's item categorization updates
Realtime subscription is cleaned up when leaving the shared list view
No duplicate realtime channels are created
Lint passes
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: Swift — Fetch Owner's Stores for Shared Lists
userStories.description: Update ListDetailViewModel and StoreService so that when viewing a shared list, the owner's stores are loaded alongside the current user's stores. Add StoreService.fetchStoresByIds(), pass ownerId into ListDetailViewModel, merge shared stores into the stores array.
acceptanceCriteria: When viewing a shared list, items are grouped under the correct store sections with name, color, and categories
The current user's own stores still load and display correctly (no regression)
Store section headers show the owner's store name and color dot
Store-specific category grouping works (owner's store categories used)
The stores array contains both own and shared stores, with shared stores properly sorted
Xcode build succeeds

userStories.title: Swift — Realtime Sync for Shared Stores
userStories.description: When the list owner modifies their stores, the shared user's view should update in realtime. Subscribe to a second stores realtime channel filtered by the owner's user_id when the list is shared. Clean up in deinit/cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: When the list owner renames a store, the shared user sees the updated name without reloading
When the list owner changes a store's color, the shared user sees the updated color
When the list owner modifies store categories, the shared user's item categorization updates
The additional realtime channel is cleaned up when leaving the view
No duplicate channels are created (guard against double subscription)
Xcode build succeeds

userStories.title: Shared Store Read-Only Guard
userStories.description: Ensure shared users cannot accidentally modify the list owner's stores. Disable edit/delete controls in React StoreManager and Swift StoreBrowserView for stores not owned by the current user. Visually distinguish shared stores from own stores.
acceptanceCriteria: React: Store Manager does not show edit/delete controls for stores not owned by the current user
Swift: Store browser does not show edit/delete controls for stores not owned by the current user
Attempting to edit/delete a shared store via API returns an error (RLS enforcement)
Shared stores are visually distinguishable from own stores (subtle indicator)
Lint passes
npm run build succeeds
Xcode build succeeds
userStories.notes: React StoreManager.jsx updated with read-only guards for shared stores. Swift StoreBrowserView naturally guarded (only shows own stores via StoreViewModel).
userStories.testIntensityReason: Permission enforcement on both platforms, but RLS is the true guard
